transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log, the 9th of November 2013.
Last night I went and saw the movie Injures a Game with my mom.
Somebody wanted me to do a video review, a movie review of it, so I guess I'll do that later on.
Today I got up at like 10. I got up that kind of late last night. I got up at like 10 today.
And I was cleaning the house. The house had been kind of getting dirty.
I'm still kind of organizing to move in.
So I was doing that. And then my mom, I went over to my mom's house and I helped her with a bunch of stuff.
Vacuumed her house. Helped her organize.
I brought a bunch of coats over there. From here I had too many over here.
And threw them in a Tupperware and threw them in the back and some random stuff like that.
And then we went over to some clothes place. I had a sale and she bought me some socks.
She wanted me to get me a bunch of like underwear, but I didn't want that.
She's always trying to buy me underwear. I've got plenty of underwear.
I've been wearing underwear for 20 years, but I've got some cool socks.
And then she came back here and I showed her like my little kayak hanging because she hadn't seen that in this new storage closet that I built for my neighbor.
And, uh, and then I came back in my house and I've been cleaning the house and I built a little extension on my bed.
So I have a bedside table right next to my, um, oh, maybe I'll show it.
Doo-dee-doo-dee-doo.
Ooh! And then I did some videos.
I did a couple videos and it's 10-15.
So today was basically like a catch-up day.
But, uh, check this out.
Yeah, this is what I made today.
It's like a little, uh, check it out.
Here, let me turn this on.
It's my bedside table.
Or it's my couchside table.
Comes right up against here.
And I can take extra weight.
And then for my bike thing, I have, uh, I got, uh, I have a JB Weld here to keep these from falling off and going away.
And this stays down.
So when I get my little bike rack, all I do is slip it right in there.
And it can take all the weight it needs, like way more than 10 pounds.
And then I have the straps.
So I have these straps that come down that I can attach to the end of the bike rack and cinch up.
So it's just on there.
And I can balance and all that kind of stuff and not break.
So I'm excited about that.
So that's basically all I did today.
I don't know if I'll have energy to go to the gym.
It was one of those catch-up organizational days.
I got rid of the rug in the kitchen, you know, because it's too dirty to clean up and all that kind of stuff.
So, uh, yeah.
So that's that.
Peace out.
